mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >55% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in both series fail to generate positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive short-term equity momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during tightening periods or 5-day forward returns show no significant difference from baseline (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate earnings expectations, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows or positive payroll surprises correlate with negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and forcing negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets price in inflation passively and forward returns remain positive in >65% of high-inflation months, invalidating the repricing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth confirm synchronized demand recovery, boosting corporate cash flow forecasts and driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth decouples from retail sales, or 60-day returns are negative in >55% of synchronized growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining inflation alongside stable federal funds rates signals a successful soft landing, removing recession risk premiums and generating positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets discount soft landing narratives and forward returns turn negative when inflation falls without rate cuts, or signal accuracy drops below 50%.
